frontmatter.title: Monoidal Categories are Bicategories With a Single Object

mainmatter.p: A monoidal category is nothing more than a bicategory with only a single object; to be more precise, given a such a bicategory \(B\) with just one object \(\bullet\in B\), the hom category \(\mathbf{hom}_{B}{\left(\bullet,\bullet\right)}\) is monoidal, with the tensor product given by horizontal composition, the tensor unit given by the horizontal identity, and unitors and associators given by those of \(B\).
A bicategory \(B\) is given by the following structure and axioms:

ol.li: for each pair of 0-cells \(x,y\) a category \(\mathbf{hom}_{B}{\left(x,y\right)}\) of morphisms or 1-cells \(x\xrightarrow{f}y\);
for each object \(x\), a morphism \(x\xrightarrow{1_{x}}x\) called the identity morphism;

for all objects \(x,y,z\), a functor \(\mathbf{hom}_{B}{\left(x,y\right)}\times\mathbf{hom}_{B}{\left(y,z\right)}\xrightarrow{{\left(\mathbin{\boldsymbol{;}}\right)}}\mathbf{hom}_{B}{\left(x,z\right)}\) called horizontal composition;

for all 1-morphisms \(e\xrightarrow{f}x\xrightarrow{g}y\xrightarrow{h}z\), an isomorphism \({\left(f\mathbin{\boldsymbol{;}} g\right)}\mathbin{\boldsymbol{;}} h \xrightarrow{\alpha_{f,g,h}} f\mathbin{\boldsymbol{;}}{\left(g\mathbin{\boldsymbol{;}} h\right)}\) natural in \(f,g,h\) called the associator;
for all 1-morphisms \(x\xrightarrow{f}y\), an isomorphism \(1_{x}\mathbin{\boldsymbol{;}} f\xrightarrow{\lambda_{f}} f\) natural in \(f\) called the left unitor;
for all 1-morphisms \(x\xrightarrow{f}y\), an isomorphism \(f\mathbin{\boldsymbol{;}}1_{y}\xrightarrow{\rho_{f}} f\) natural in \(f\) called the right unitor;
such that the triangle below commutes for all 1-morphisms \(x\xrightarrow{f}y\xrightarrow{g}z\):
and the pentagon below commutes for all 1-morphisms \(v\xrightarrow{e}w\xrightarrow{f}x\xrightarrow{g}y\xrightarrow{h}z\):

mainmatter.p: A monoidal category is a category \(C\) equipped with the following structure and axioms:

ol.li: a functor \(C\times C\xrightarrow{\otimes}C\) called the tensor product;
an object \(I\in C\) called the tensor unit;
a natural isomorphism \({\left(x\otimes y\right)}\otimes z \xrightarrow{\alpha_{x,y,z}} x\otimes{\left(y\otimes z\right)}\) called the associator;
a natural isomorphism \(I\otimes x \xrightarrow{\lambda_{x}}x\) called the left unitor;
a natural isomorphism \(x\otimes I \xrightarrow{\rho_{x}}x\) called the right unitor;
such that the triangle below commutes:
